name: Rural Livelihoods & Development
student_summary: Build livelihoods in villages — SHGs, NRLM, development programmes.
overview: Build livelihoods in villages — SHGs, NRLM, development programmes. Rural Livelihoods & Development sits in the Food Systems family of Agriculture, Food & Environment. It is a broad career direction — you can enter it through several routes and specialize later without restarting.
who_is_this_for: You want work that serves your community or country
who_should_avoid: You want a laptop-anywhere lifestyle — this work happens in person
daily_responsibilities: Apply community mobilization to real problems
Collaborate with your team using programme management
Keep improving at livelihood design — the field keeps moving
Communicate progress, results and decisions to the people who depend on them
work_environment: Primarily on-site — offices, facilities or the field depending on the role
problem_solving_style: People-centred — listen, mediate, guide and align humans
scope: Demand outlook in India is booming; see FAQs for how the platform keeps this current.
advantages: Demand is booming: opportunities across India and beyond
A broad Food Systems foundation that opens multiple specializations later
challenges: Location-bound work; postings and relocation are part of the deal
Early years demand patience before the rewards compound
misconceptions: “You must pick your exact job title now.” Rural Livelihoods & Development is a direction — specialization comes later.
“Only toppers make it.” Consistent skill-building beats one exam rank in the long run.
portfolio_ideas: A documented case study of programme management
projects: Beginner: explore community mobilization with a small self-chosen project
Intermediate: build or run something real using MIS systems
smaller_city_scope: Viable in smaller cities — demand exists well beyond the metros.
rural_scope: Real rural relevance — this field serves or operates in rural India directly.
home_state_note: Ask the AI coach about your state — regional demand, cities and relocation advice are generated live from retrieved data, never hardcoded.
transition_paths: Toward Social Work: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward NGO & Development Management: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward Agri-business & Rural Entrepreneurship: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
faqs: What does Rural Livelihoods & Development pay in India?
Entry roles typically start around ₹4 LPA and senior professionals reach ₹12 LPA or more. Live, region-specific salary data is retrieved by the platform when you ask.
What should I study after Class 12?
Focus on Social Science, Agriculture. Typical degrees: Any degree + rural management (IRMA), M.A. Development. Alternatives exist: diploma in agriculture + field experience.
Will AI take over this career?
AI impact is minimal — deeply human/physical work. Broad paths like Rural Livelihoods & Development evolve rather than disappear — the students who pair domain skill with AI tools benefit most.
Is this a broad path or a specific job?
Rural Livelihoods & Development is a career direction. Once inside it you can specialize further — the platform will map specializations in future versions.
